title: Question: Internal Error: Function not underneath a statement
body:
---


Author Name: **R. Diez**
Original Redmine Message: 897 from https://www.veripool.org



---

I have defined this little helper function:

    function automatic [31:0] pc_addr_to_32;
       input reg [31:2] pc_addr;
       begin
          pc_addr_to_32 = { pc_addr, 2'b00 };
       end
    endfunction

If I try to use it like this:

  $display( "0x%08h", pc_addr_to_32( 0 ) );

I get the following error:

  %Error: Internal Error: /home/blah/blah/:2005: ../V3Task.cpp:990: Function not underneath a statement

I have to resort to a temporary variable to make it work:

  temp = pc_addr_to_32( 0 );
  $display( "0x%08h", temp );

I am using the latest Verilator sources, but that happens with older versions too.

Thanks in advance,
  rdiez
